_note: Queued by Lyra (synthesis lead, sproutlab Province) on 2026-05-08. Drafts canon-gov-013 formalizing the canon-rule correction protocol — when a canon-rule defect is found in a Province-authored brief, the affected author posts the correction (path 1). Sovereign-routed to gov-family per the 2026-05-07 in-session ratification on the originating case (Phase 1 brief defect on rishabh1804/sproutlab PR #52, Maren-discovered, Aurelius-corrected). Source of truth for the convention: rishabh1804/sproutlab issue #58 body. Audit chain on this PR: Cipher Edict V cross-cut → Aurelius final pass → Sovereign approval → merge. On merge, sproutlab issue #58 closes with forward-link to this canon entry. Hygiene observation (out of scope for this PR): canon-gov-011 currently has a slug collision on main between canon-gov-011-merge-is-the-deploy-step and canon-gov-011-priest-office-embassy-rites — flagged for separate Sovereign-routed cleanup.

canons.title: Canon-Rule Correction Protocol: Affected Author Posts the Amendment
canons.rationale:
**Convention.** When a canon-rule defect is found in a brief authored by a specific Province lead, the affected author posts the correction amendment (path 1). The discoverer flags the defect; the author owns the correction. Voices stay unmixed in the record; the authorship chain is preserved across audit-chain artifacts.

**Rationale.** Three reinforcing reasons. (1) Authorship-chain preservation — future readers tracing brief evolution see one voice per artifact, not interleaved attribution. (2) Ownership — the author who got the canon shape wrong owns the correction; this reinforces the audit-chain accountability already established by canon-gov-002 (Governors activate during QA only) and canon-cc-008 (Censor runs after Governors, not in parallel). (3) Discoverer's lane stays clean — the discoverer's primary work (e.g. a Governor's polish thread) does not accumulate cross-Province corrections that bury its substance.

**When to apply.** Canon-rule defects in Aurelius-authored briefs (audit-chain reviews, Phase briefs, conformance checks); Builder mis-attribution in Lyra-authored synthesis artifacts; wrong-Province-pen mis-assignment in any audit-chain document. Generally: any defect in a Province-authored artifact that affects the canon shape (lead assignment, audit-chain ordering, Province scope) rather than the artifact's substance.

**Exceptions.** Two carve-outs. (a) Author unavailable in-session — path (2), in which the discoverer posts the correction with explicit cross-link to the discovery and the canon basis, is acceptable; the affected author folds an acknowledgment when next reachable. (b) Substance defects, not canon defects — if the discoverer is also the natural reviewer of the substance (e.g. Cipher Edict V cross-cut surfacing a structural issue in a brief Cipher would normally cross-cut anyway), the cross-cut comment IS the authoritative record; no amendment chain is needed. Path (1) applies specifically to canon-shape defects where the discoverer is outside the artifact's normal review path.

**Originating case.** SproutLab PR #52 / PR #56 design-grounding workstream, 2026-05-07. Original Phase 1 brief on PR #52 (comment 4395444408) listed Phase 2 as 'Cipher (lead) + Maren' and Phase 3 as 'Cipher + Lyra' — both off-canon, since Cipher is a cross-cluster Censor under canon-cc-008, not a Province lead under canon-gov-002. Maren's Phase 1 polish thread on PR #56 (comment 4395821623) flagged the canon mismatch with a two-path offer to Sovereign. Sovereign in-session ratified path (1) as default and memorialized the convention for forward recurrence. Aurelius posted the correction on PR #52 (comment 4397164732) citing canon-gov-002 + canon-cc-008. SproutLab issue #58 captured the convention durably; this canon promotes it from issue-record to formal codex registry under the gov-family lane Sovereign routed.

**Promotion lane.** Issue #58's body anticipated three plausible promotion lanes: gov-family (governance), cc-family (cross-cluster), or a new aud-family (audit-chain). Sovereign routed to gov-family on the grounds that the convention governs how Provinces interact with each other's authored artifacts under the existing audit-chain ordering — squarely within the governance lane already populated by canon-gov-002 and canon-cc-008. A future canon-aud-NNN family, if instituted, may supersede this entry; supersession would follow the standard amendment path.

**Amendment path.** Standard gov-family amendment — Working-ratification under canon-cc-014 is available; Sovereign-direct ratification is also valid. Supersession by a future canon-aud-NNN entry is permitted on the same path.
